source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI. Cover page confirms Turf Holdings Inc. 2025 FDD for WEED MAN franchise. Issuance date: March 17, 2025. Weed Man also offers mosquito control and perimeter pest services (added 2019) but is classified in the lawn_turf_treatment wedge based on primary service line.

brand.parent_company: #1051080 Ontario Inc. / TH Canada Inc. (master licensor)

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Fee varies by territory population: $30,000 for up to 150,000 people (1 Unit Territory); $50,000 for 150,001-300,000 people (2 Unit Territories). Most new grants are for 2 Unit Territories. Additional Unit Territories: $30,000 each. Nonrefundable upon payment (except if government license cannot be obtained, refund less greater of $2,500 or actual costs). Seasonal discounts: 10% off if paid in full by July 31; 7% by Sept 30; 5% by Nov 30. Minimum $12,000 deposit reserves territory for 30 days. Training fee of $4,600 is separate (see Item 7). WEMMS.net software $4,635 first year is also separate.

provenance.exact_text: You must pay an Initial Franchise Fee of $30,000 to $50,000 when you sign the Franchise Agreement.

royalty_rate.notes: Tiered: 6.5% on annual cumulative Net Sales up to $1,000,000; 5.5% on annual cumulative Net Sales above $1,000,000. Minimum annual royalty: $7,192.46 per Unit Territory (2025), adjusted annually for CPI inflation from Nov 1, 2023 base. If multi-territory, minimum is multiplied by number of Unit Territories. New royalty structure introduced 2023; ~95% of system converted Jan 1, 2024. 'Net Sales' = actual gross sales of all services invoiced plus all other revenues, excluding taxes and bartered fair market value.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Royalty Fee row and Notes 1-2
provenance.exact_text: 6.5% of Net Sales... 5.5% on any calendar year Net Sales in excess of $1,000,000

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Single advertising fund contribution of 1.2% of Net Sales. May increase to up to 3% after December 31, 2033. Franchisor matches 50% of franchisee contributions until December 31, 2033.

components.name: Advertising Fund Contribution
components.notes: Payable at same time as monthly Royalty Fee via ACH. May increase to up to 3% after Dec 31, 2033. Franchisor contributes 50% match until Dec 31, 2033.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Unusually simple and low marketing obligation compared to other brands in both wedges. 1.2% is the lowest advertising fund rate in the lawn cohort. Franchisor's 50% match is a notable franchisee-friendly provision.

provenance.exact_text: Each month you will contribute 1.2% of Net Sales.

technology_fee.notes: WEMMS.net software: $4,635 first year (includes software + first year support). Subsequent years: 0.65% of previous year's gross sales, minimum $1,200/year. Example: $1M sales → $6,500/year ($541.67/month). Paid to third-party provider chosen by franchisor. Also QuickBooks ~$115 and computer hardware ~$1,500.

provenance.exact_text: the cost of support for this computer software will be calculated at 0.65% of the previous year's gross sales with a minimum cost of $1200/year

transfer_fee.formula: $10,000 plus legal costs and expenses, including administrative costs, legal fees, and training costs.

provenance.exact_text: Transfer fee of $10,000 plus legal costs and expenses

other_fees.notes: 50% of the then-current initial franchise fee.
$450-$550 seminar fee plus all travel and living expenses.
If franchisee requires additional advice or guidance beyond standard support.
Reasonable fee plus expenses, upon death or incapacity, or if no competent manager available.
$100-$1,000. Travel and personal expenses of auditor if reports not submitted or royalties understated by more than 3%.

line_items.notes: Online or in-person in Orono, Ontario. Includes materials, instructors, up to 12 hours software training.

line_items.category: Travel & Living Expenses for Initial Training

line_items.notes: Office equipment, safety equipment, uniforms, fertilizer spreader, measuring wheel, pest control mister sprayer (~$1,000), hand sprayer ($150-$250).
360-gallon spray tank + truck (10,000 lb GVWR). Monthly lease ~$1,000-$1,500. Spray package can be purchased separately for $12,000-$14,000 if franchisee owns conforming truck.
WEMMS.net software $4,635 (first year), QuickBooks ~$115, computer hardware ~$1,500.
First 3 months estimate. Annual premiums typically $4,000-$8,000.
Utility deposits, business licenses, security deposits, incorporation fees. Does not include insurance.
Includes payroll (not owner salary), lease payments (not debt service). Not a break-even estimate.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 explicitly states: 'We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised units.' No tables, no financial data disclosed.

provenance.exact_text: We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised units.

years_reported.notes: All outlets are franchised. No company-owned outlets. Net growth +9. Item 20 counts franchise agreements, not physical locations.
All outlets are franchised. Net growth +14. 1 termination (FL), 1 non-renewal (TX), 1 ceased operations (PA).

CRITICAL CONTEXT: 2024 start count (117) reflects massive contract merging effective January 1, 2024, NOT actual system contraction. In 2023, ~95% of franchisees converted to new franchise agreements and many multi-area contracts were merged. As of Dec 31, 2024: 154 physical franchisee locations, 121 franchise agreements ('Areas'), 274 franchise areas, and 759 Unit Territories. The Supplemental Table is the authoritative system-size metric for 2024. Net change of -134 in Table 1 is an artifact of contract consolidation, not loss of franchisees or territories.

provenance.exact_text: These figures were affected by the merging of many franchise agreements with existing franchisees that became effective on January 1, 2024.

item_3_litigation.notes: No litigation required to be disclosed. No bankruptcy either (Item 4).

provenance.exact_text: No litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.
We are a Delaware corporation incorporated in July 1995... TH Canada and its predecessor have conducted a business of the type franchised since 1970.

warnings: Royalty is tiered (6.5% up to $1M, 5.5% above) — recorded with field_status 'ambiguous'. New royalty structure introduced 2023; ~95% of system converted Jan 1, 2024.
Item 19: NO financial performance representations made. This is the only lawn brand so far to decline Item 19 disclosure.

Item 20 2024 data is heavily affected by contract merging. Table 1 shows -134 net change but this is an artifact of consolidation, not system contraction. Supplemental Table shows 154 physical locations, 759 territories.
Technology fee is revenue-based (0.65% of prior year gross sales, min $1,200/year after first year). Cannot be expressed as a fixed monthly amount.
Canadian-origin system (1970/1976). US franchisor entity (Turf Holdings Inc.) incorporated 1995. System age calculated from US franchising start (1995).
Weed Man also offers mosquito control and perimeter pest services (added 2019) — cross-wedge service overlap, but classified in lawn_turf_treatment based on primary service.
Franchisor merged with many sub-franchisors effective January 1, 2025. Now 82.6% owned by parent #1051080 Ontario Inc.
Real estate costs excluded from Item 7 total because financing method is too variable to estimate.
Advertising fund rate (1.2%) is the lowest in the lawn cohort. Franchisor's 50% match through 2033 is a notable pro-franchisee provision.

null_fields.reason: Fee varies by territory population ($30K for ≤150K pop, $50K for >150K pop).
Revenue-based: 0.65% of prior year gross sales, minimum $1,200/year. Not a fixed amount.
